source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals.

confidence_rationale: Pfeiffer & Foster demonstrate that hippocampal place-cell sequences encode trajectories from current location to remembered goal locations *before* navigation begins, supporting the broader claim that hippocampal sequences carry planning content (path proposals from current state to goal). This supports MECH-294's framing that state_summary, goal_latent, and an implicit action_proposal (the trajectory) co-exist in hippocampal sequence content. Confidence 0.70: the paper's primary sequences are SWR-associated rather than theta-cycle sequences, so the binding-window evidence is for a different oscillatory regime than MECH-294's theta-cycle window. This is a partial-support / oblique-evidence entry.
failure_signatures: The hippocampal sequences here are sharp-wave-ripple (SWR) replay events, not theta-cycle sequences. SWR events are 100-200ms compressed sequences during pauses; theta cycles are 100-150ms cycles during active behaviour. The two oscillatory regimes have different content and different downstream consumers, so the paper's evidence is oblique to MECH-294's theta-cycle binding-window claim.

The streams co-encoded in the sequence are start location and goal location -- both spatial -- with the action proposal implicit in the trajectory shape. MECH-294 needs four explicit semantically-distinct streams. The Pfeiffer & Foster evidence implicates two-stream binding at most.
The paper does not address risk content at all. Whether risk_estimate would be co-encoded in the same hippocampal sequences is unaddressed.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, hippocampal place-cell sequences encode brief spatial trajectories strongly biased to progress from the rat's current location to a known goal location. These sequences are predictive of immediate future behaviour, generalise to novel start-goal combinations, and identify behaviourally relevant paths at moments of memory retrieval.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-294 takes Pfeiffer & Foster as evidence that hippocampal sequence content jointly encodes start-state and goal-location, with an implicit action-trajectory connecting them. This is a two-stream-with-implicit-action analogue of MECH-294's four-stream packet. The paper supports the substrate-level claim that hippocampal sequences carry planning content (state + goal + trajectory) jointly readable by downstream consumers.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Pfeiffer & Foster sequences are SWR-associated, not theta-cycle sequences. SWR replay and theta sequences are different oscillatory regimes with different consumers, so the paper's evidence is oblique to MECH-294's theta-cycle binding-window claim. The streams demonstrated are state + goal + implicit-trajectory -- three streams -- but all spatial; risk is not addressed.
mapping.source_context: Direct CA1 unit recording in rats performing a goal-directed open-arena task. Nature, 2013. Foster lab is a leading hippocampal-replay group.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature, direct unit recording). Mapping fidelity moderate because the joint-content evidence is real but in the SWR regime rather than the theta-cycle regime MECH-294 needs. Transfer risk elevated because of the SWR-vs-theta regime difference -- the binding-window architectures may not be the same.
